title: NZ School Holidays Travel Tips — How to Beat the Crowds and Save Money
meta_desc: Discover how to save money and avoid crowds during NZ school holidays with our expert travel tips
intro: If you're planning to travel during NZ school holidays, be prepared for peak prices and crowds. However, with some insider knowledge, you can beat the crowds and save money. For example, did you know that booking your accommodation at least 6 months in advance can save you up to 30%?

sections.heading: Understanding Peak and Off-Peak Pricing
sections.body: Peak season in NZ typically falls during school holidays, with prices for accommodation and activities increasing by up to 50%. For instance, a night at the Novotel Queenstown Lakeside can cost around $450 during peak season, compared to $280 during off-peak season.

sections.heading: Tips for Avoiding the Crowds
list: Visit popular attractions early in the morning or late in the evening
Consider visiting lesser-known destinations, such as the Coromandel Peninsula or the Hawke's Bay region
Book a guided tour to avoid queues and make the most of your time

sections.heading: Booking Lead Times
sections.body: It's essential to book your accommodation and activities well in advance, especially during peak season. For example, booking a campervan with Jucy Campervans at least 3 months in advance can save you up to 20% on your rental fees.

sections.heading: Transportation Costs
list: Renting a car for a week can cost around $400-$600, depending on the rental company and vehicle type
Fuel costs can range from $100-$200 per week, depending on your driving distance and vehicle fuel efficiency
Consider using public transport or shuttle services to reduce your transportation costs

sections.heading: Budgeting for Your Trip
sections.body: To get an estimate of your trip costs, use our NZ Family Travel Budget Calculator. This tool allows you to input your travel dates, accommodation options, and activities to get a detailed breakdown of your expenses.

sections.heading: Popular Destinations
sections.body: Some popular destinations in NZ include Queenstown, Rotorua, and Auckland. Each of these destinations offers a range of activities and attractions, from skiing and hiking to cultural experiences and city tours. The drive from Auckland to Rotorua takes around 3 hours, while the drive from Queenstown to Wanaka takes around 1 hour.
